description: Comprehensive Mimir admin dashboard for error analysis, sample rejection tracking, and operational health across write/read paths.

panels.description: Overall error rate as a percentage of all requests. Combines 4xx and 5xx responses divided by total request volume.
panels.title: Total Error Rate %

panels.description: Rate of 5xx server errors per second across all Mimir routes. Indicates internal failures, timeouts, or upstream service issues.
panels.title: 5xx Errors/sec

panels.description: Rate of 4xx client errors per second. Includes bad requests (400), unauthorized (401), rate limited (429), etc.
panels.title: 4xx Errors/sec

panels.description: Total request throughput across all Mimir routes and status codes.
panels.title: Total Request Rate

panels.description: Percentage of requests returning 2xx success status codes. Target is 99%+ for healthy operations.
panels.title: Success Rate %

panels.description: P95 request latency across all Mimir routes. Lower is better — high values indicate slow queries or write contention.
panels.title: P95 Latency

panels.description: Breakdown of all 4xx and 5xx errors by HTTP status code over time. Helps identify specific error types — 429 rate limits, 500 internal errors, 502 bad gateways, etc.
panels.title: Errors by HTTP Status Code

panels.description: Comparison of 5xx server error rate vs 4xx client error rate as percentages of total traffic. The 1% threshold line indicates the target ceiling for acceptable error rates.
panels.title: 5xx vs 4xx Error Rate %

panels.description: Top 10 API routes generating 5xx server errors. Helps pinpoint which endpoints are experiencing internal failures.
panels.title: Top 10 Routes — 5xx Errors

panels.description: Top 10 API routes generating 4xx client errors. Useful for identifying misconfigured clients, unauthorized access attempts, or rate-limited tenants.
panels.title: Top 10 Routes — 4xx Errors

panels.description: 5xx server error rate per cluster as a percentage of total requests. The 1% threshold marks the boundary for acceptable error rates per cluster.
panels.title: 5xx Error Rate by Cluster

panels.description: Absolute error volume (4xx + 5xx combined) per cluster over time. Useful for capacity planning and identifying clusters under unusual load.
panels.title: Error Volume by Cluster

panels.description: Rate of discarded samples broken down by reason. Common reasons include out-of-order samples, rate limits, label validation failures, and per-user series limits.
panels.title: Discarded Samples by Reason

panels.description: Rate of HTTP 429 rate-limited requests across all routes. Spikes indicate tenants hitting ingestion or query rate limits.
panels.title: Rate Limited Requests (429)

panels.description: Top 10 tenants/users by discarded sample rate. Identifies the noisiest tenants causing the most sample rejections.
panels.title: Discarded Samples by User/Tenant

panels.description: Top 15 API routes ranked by total request volume. Identifies the busiest endpoints across the Mimir deployment.
panels.title: Top 15 Routes by Volume

panels.description: Request rate over time broken down by route. Stacked view shows total throughput composition and how traffic distributes across endpoints.
panels.title: Request Rate by Route

panels.description: Error rate on the write path (push routes) as a percentage of total push requests. Captures both 4xx and 5xx errors. The 1% threshold marks the operational target ceiling.
panels.title: Write Path Error Rate

panels.description: Write path errors broken down by HTTP status code. Shows the composition of push failures — distinguishing between client errors (4xx) and server errors (5xx) on the ingestion path.
panels.title: Write Errors by Status Code

panels.description: Error rate on the read/query path as a percentage of total query requests. Covers query, query_range, label, and series endpoints. The 1% threshold marks the operational target.
panels.title: Query Frontend Error Rate

panels.description: Rate of query frontend enqueue failures with 4xx/5xx status codes. Indicates queue saturation or request rejections at the query frontend scheduler.
panels.title: Query Frontend Queue Rejections

panels.description: Top 10 API routes ranked by P95 request latency. Identifies the slowest endpoints that may need query optimization or resource scaling.
panels.title: P95 Latency by Route

panels.description: Latency percentile distribution across all Mimir routes over time. Shows P50, P90, P95, and P99 to understand the full latency spectrum and identify tail-latency issues.
panels.title: Latency Percentiles — All

title: Mimir Admin — Errors, Rejections & Operations
